mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real single-family housing sales and new housing starts indicate strengthening residential demand and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which consistently produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions correlate with negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation trigger months despite subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real gross domestic product and real single-family housing sales confirms broad-based economic expansion and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a coefficient insignificant at the 90 percent confidence level in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal contracting manufacturing activity and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of rising interest rates.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 25 basis points in the prior quarter.
